nodes.label: surviving recipe: [SCFF x k -> GD-readout] x N (read, few blocks)
nodes.rationale: The Phase-2 deliverable: forward-only single-sample per-sample-normalized SCFF bulk (0 backward) + tiny sleep-consolidated GD readout heads, boosted via logit-sum (read), never write. Continual-safe by construction.

nodes.label: P2.6 — the substrate filter + the continual veto (closes Phase 2)
nodes.rationale: Re-runs the boosted-read recipe (N=4 blocks, k=1) through the class-incremental digits stream vs single-block baseline, GD-online catastrophic baseline, and a no-sleep rot control. 3 seeds.

nodes.label: P2.6 verdict: VETO PASSED — Phase 2 CLOSED
nodes.rationale: boosted-read+sleep final 0.932 (BWT -0.034) approx single-block 0.938 (-0.026), far above rot 0.332 (-0.80) and GD-online 0.186 (-0.99). SCFF probe stays flat (doesn't forget); sleep is decisive.

nodes.label: SUBSTRATE table (recipe -> chip cost)
nodes.rationale: per-block SCFF = weight crossbar, per-sample norm (no batch/running stats), backward credit=0, online + continual-safe; GD readouts = small head matrices, online + sleep-consolidated; write mode rejected on both cost and accuracy axes.

nodes.label: Decision: Phase 2 closes; recipe hands off to Phase 3
nodes.rationale: Depth = block-count not SCFF-layer-count. Drift is measured (compounds slightly with blocks) but the gate itself is deferred to the maintenance phase.
